title: Create and monitor a study
description: Configure a study, watch the trials fill in live, and read the terminal state — the core Karpathy loop.

screenshots.caption: Open the Studies page. Every study you create lands here with its status, cluster, query set, and trial count. The status filter chips at the top scope by lifecycle stage.

Click 'queued' to filter the list to studies waiting for the orchestrator. The URL updates to `?status=queued` so the filter is sharable + bookmarkable. Studies move queued → running → (completed | failed | cancelled) as the orchestrator processes them.

Click 'Create study' to open the multi-step modal. Step 1 binds the study to a cluster + target index. Subsequent steps pick the query set + judgment list, the query template, the search-space (which params Optuna will vary + their ranges), and the objective metric (ndcg@10, precision@k, etc.) + budget (max_trials or time_budget_min).

The detail page is where the operator spends real time. Header surfaces the study name, status badge, baseline metric, and best-so-far metric. The trials table below populates in real-time as Optuna's TPE sampler proposes params and the worker scores them via `pytrec_eval`. The page auto-polls every 3 seconds while the study is running.

Once max_trials is reached (or the operator cancels via the 'Cancel study' button, which fires `POST /api/v1/studies/{id}/cancel`), the status moves to a terminal state and the orchestrator emits a proposal + digest. Cancellation is graceful — in-flight trials complete cleanly; no new trials enqueue.
